free_text: Differential cross sections for the T(p,n)3He reaction
- final data of T(p,n)3He reaction
- prelim.data of T(p,n);
T(p,n+p) and D(d,n+p) reactions
'Neutrons from p+T and D+D',
W.E.Wilson, Theses, University of Wisconsin
- full description of the experiment
Compilation produced by Arzamas RFNC-VNIIEF
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. BIB updated
Subents 005-007 added.
The tritium gas was contained in a thin-walled steel
cylinder 2 cm long and 0.6 cm in diam. Target
pressures were such that the tritium gas caused 5 and
13 MeV protons to lose 40 and 30 keV of energy,
respectively.
The stilbene crystal was 0.6 cm thick and
and 25 mm in diameter, mounted on an RCA6342A
photomultiplier tube, was used as the detector.
The angle subtended by the crystal at the
target was about 4.5 degr.
Total error
statistical error

(same figs 9,10 in Wilson's thesis)

SD: From prev.compilation (A1305.002 and
A1600.003). Data were re-digitized.
The tritium gas was contained in a thin-walled steel
cylinder 2 cm long and 0.6 cm in diam. Target
pressures were such that the tritium gas caused 5 and
13 MeV protons to lose 40 and 30 keV of energy,
respectively.
By authors. Using the spectrometer properties of the
counter telescope, an attempt was made to observe the
continuum of neutrons from the reaction T(p,np)D.
At abombarding energy of 11.9 MeV no neutrons were
observed that could definitely be  attributed to
the reaction and the upper limit placed on the
differential cross section at zero degrees is 10 mb/sr.
Text on p.429 of Nucl.Phys.,27(1961)421
SD: From prev.compilation (A1600.004).
The tritium gas was contained in a thin-walled steel
cylinder 2 cm long and 0.6 cm in diam. Target
pressures were such that the tritium gas caused 5 and
13 MeV protons to lose 40 and 30 keV of energy,
respectively.
Recoil proton counter telescope.
Argon with 5% CO2 was used as the proportional
counter gas. The counter wires were .125 mm mo1ybdenum.
- Because
the composition of the target gas was unknown in the
present case, the present work is a relative
measurement of the differential cross section and is
normalized to the data of Perry et al. in the region
of 5 to 6 MeV.

Bull.Am.Phys.Soc.5,410(D6),1960
Total error shown in a few points on fig.
The error bars shown are +- 7% which is
the estimated error of the data of Perry et al.
